dataset: Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions Database (University of Liverpool & Radboudumc)

pi_items.source: FDA approval records, MedlinePlus, Mayo Clinic, Wikipedia, DrugBank, Cancer Research UK - all confirm Sorafenib is a tyrosine kinase inhibitor used for advanced renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, thyroid cancer, and AML
MedlinePlus, Mayo Clinic, NHS, DrugBank, WebMD - all confirm Metformin is a biguanide antidiabetic medication used as first-line treatment for type 2 diabetes
Code analysis: export_result.sh script (line 7) defines RESULT_PATH="/sdcard/interaction_result.txt" and checks for this file

pi_items.verified_value: Official Liverpool Cancer iChart documentation describes traffic light system as: red, amber, yellow, and green. However, the metadata lists 'orange' instead of 'amber' and adds 'grey'. The validated_pi.json from the check_antidepressant_tamoxifen_safety task (a similar task in the same environment) also uses the same color list with 'orange' and 'grey', suggesting this may be how the app displays or labels the colors, though official documentation consistently mentions 'amber' not 'orange'
pi_items.source: Web search of cancer-druginteractions.org archived site, official App Store and Google Play descriptions, Liverpool HEP and HIV iChart apps (same developer) documentation, and comparison with validated_pi.json from check_antidepressant_tamoxifen_safety@1 task

pi_items.metadata_value: Task asks agent to check interaction (implicit claim that interaction exists and is recorded)
pi_items.verified_value: TRUE - Sorafenib and Metformin interaction is well-documented in clinical literature
pi_items.source: PubMed, PMC, ScienceDirect, Molecular Pharmacology journal - multiple peer-reviewed studies document drug-drug interaction with documented clinical consequences

pi_items.metadata_value: Not specified in metadata (task does not specify expected interaction color)
pi_items.verified_value: Clinical evidence indicates caution/contraindication is warranted. Patients on sorafenib + metformin have 4-5 months shorter overall survival compared to sorafenib alone in hepatocellular carcinoma. Metformin reduces sorafenib AUC by 40% (AUC ratio 0.6). Mechanism: both drugs inhibit mTOR pathway, potentially causing sorafenib resistance
pi_items.source: PubMed PMID: 31489317, 33990407; Molecular Pharmacology 2024; Hepatobiliary Surgery and Nutrition (Orsi et al.); Multiple HCC cell line studies (PMC4784359, PMC6125270, PMC9329836)

privileged_info_summary: The task involves checking the drug-drug interaction between Sorafenib (a tyrosine kinase inhibitor cancer drug used for renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, thyroid cancer, and AML) and Metformin (a first-line antidiabetic agent for type 2 diabetes) in the Liverpool Cancer iChart mobile application. This is a real, well-documented interaction: clinical evidence shows patients treated with both drugs together have 4-5 months shorter overall survival compared to sorafenib alone, and metformin reduces sorafenib exposure by 40% (AUC ratio 0.6) through competing inhibition of the mTOR pathway. The Liverpool Cancer iChart app is a legitimate mobile drug interaction checker developed by the University of Liverpool's Drug Interactions Group and Radboudumc, displaying interactions using a traffic light system. The underlying data comes from the real, publicly-available Cancer Drug Interactions database (cancer-druginteractions.org), which was last updated in June 2022. The data is not synthetic but based on clinical pharmacology evidence and peer-reviewed research.
